label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking if you follow the instructions, will you return to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward, take 8 steps right, then take 8 steps left. The options are Yes or No. First, let me visualize this. If you're facing forward the whole time, then when you take 8 steps to the right, you move 8 units to the right. Then, when you take 8 steps to the left, you move 8 units to the left. So if you start at position 0, moving right 8 steps would take you to position +8. Then moving left 8 steps would take you back to 0. So in that case, you would be back to the starting point. Wait, but maybe there's a trick here. Let me think again. If you are always facing forward, then when you turn right or left, you are turning relative to your current facing direction. Wait, but the instructions say "always face forward." Hmm. Does that mean you never turn? Or does it mean that when you are moving, you are always facing forward? Wait, the wording is "always face forward. Take 8 steps right. Take 8 steps left." So the first instruction is to always face forward, meaning you never turn. So you start facing forward. Then you take 8 steps to the right. Wait, but if you're always facing forward, how can you take steps to the right? If you're facing forward, then taking a step to the right would actually be turning 90 degrees to the right and then moving forward. But the instruction says "always face forward," which might mean you don't turn. So maybe the problem is ambiguous. Alternatively, perhaps "always face forward" means that you are always facing in the same direction as you move.
